catalyst.source: The photocatalytic activities of the catalysts were evaluated by the reduction of CO 2 in methanol solution to form methyl formate.
catalyst.context: The polymeric graphite-like carbon nitride (g-C 3 N 4 ) and CdSg-C 3 N 4 composites of different mass fractions were synthesized via polycondensation and hydrothermal methods, respectively.XRD, SEM-EDS, TEM, UV-vis and PL were applied to characterize the structure, morphology and optical properties of the as-prepared CdSg-C 3 N 4 composites.The photocatalytic activities of the catalysts were evaluated by the reduction of CO 2 in methanol solution to form methyl formate.The results indicated that the photocatalytic activities of the CdSg-C 3 N 4 composites were improved significantly compared with that of the pure CdS and g-C 3 N 4 .The highest activity appeared at mass ratio 1:2 of CdS:g-C 3 N 4 .

catalyst.source: The results indicated that the photocatalytic activities of the CdSg-C 3 N 4 composites were improved significantly compared with that of the pure CdS and g-C 3 N 4 .
catalyst.context: The polymeric graphite-like carbon nitride (g-C 3 N 4 ) and CdSg-C 3 N 4 composites of different mass fractions were synthesized via polycondensation and hydrothermal methods, respectively.XRD, SEM-EDS, TEM, UV-vis and PL were applied to characterize the structure, morphology and optical properties of the as-prepared CdSg-C 3 N 4 composites.The photocatalytic activities of the catalysts were evaluated by the reduction of CO 2 in methanol solution to form methyl formate.The results indicated that the photocatalytic activities of the CdSg-C 3 N 4 composites were improved significantly compared with that of the pure CdS and g-C 3 N 4 .The highest activity appeared at mass ratio 1:2 of CdS:g-C 3 N 4 .

catalyst.source: The CdSg-C 3 N 4 photocatalysts were synthesized by a facile hydrothermal progress.
catalyst.context: All of the reagents, urea, cadmium nitrate (Cd(NO 3 ) 2 4H 2 O), thioacetamide, and methanol were commercially available and used without further purification. The polymeric graphite-like carbon nitride (g-C 3 N 4 ) was synthesized by simple pyrolysis of urea without the assistance of a template 32.In a typical synthesis, 15 g of urea powder in a quartz crucible with a cover were dried at 80 C for 4 h, then heated at 550 C for 3 h at a rate of 10 C min -1 in a muffle furnace.After the muffle furnace naturally cooled down to room temperature, the product of light yellow in the crucible was collected and ground into powder for further use. The CdSg-C 3 N 4 photocatalysts were synthesized by a facile hydrothermal progress.First, 0.1 g of g-C 3 N 4 powder was added into a 100 mL round-bottomed flask containing 30 mL deionized water and magnetically stirred for 1 h.Then, 7 mL of 0.1 M Cd(NO 3 ) 2 4H 2 O aqueous solution and 7 mL of 0.15 M thioacetamide aqueous solution were transferred into the suspension containing g-C 3 N 4 powder, then, the round bottomed flask was connected to circulating water, and the seam was sealed with teflon thin films.Subsequently, the CdSg-C 3 N 4 composite with the mass ratio of 1:1 was obtained under continuous stirring for 5 h at 90 C. Finally, the product was centrifuged, washed with deionized water and ethanol respectively for several times and dried at 60 C for 24 h.In the same procedure, the CdSg-C 3 N 4 photocatalysts with various mass ratios of 1:2, 1:3, 1:4, 1:5 were synthesized.The pure CdS was prepared under the same condition except adding g-C 3 N 4 . The crystalline phases of CdSg-C 3 N 4 composites were analyzed by powder X-ray diffraction (XRD, DMax-2500, Rigaku with Cu X-ray radiation) at a scanning speed of 4 min -1 in range of 2A 10 -75 .The morphologies and structure were examined by field emission scanning electron microscope (FE-SEM, ZEISS-Merlin compact), and transmission electron microscopy (TEM, Tecnai G2 F20).The chemical composition of the samples was determined by a SEM energy dispersion spectrum (EDS).The UV-vis diffuse reflectance spectra were captured by Shimadzu UV-2550 spectrophotometer using BaSO 4 as a reference in wavelength of 200-800 nm.Photoluminescence (PL) spectra of the samples were measured on the Fluorescence Spectphotometer (Hitachi F-7000).The samples were excited at a wavelength of 325 nm to measure the emission spectrum.The wavelength and strength of light source was measured by spectrum analyzer (Taiwan UPRtek MK350). The photocatalytic activity of the CdSg-C 3 N 4 with various mass ratios was evaluated by photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 in a batch slurry reactor 33, where light transmitted a quartz window from above and cooling water circulated in jacket, and also two connectors for bubbling CO 2 into the catalyst suspended methanol solution to saturation.The reaction temperature was controlled at 20 3 C by using a thermostatic water bath.And the reactor was illuminated by a 250 W lamp with the main wavelength of 365 nm.In one test, 20 mg photocatalyst and 20 mL methanol were added into the reactor.CO 2 was bubbled into the slurry of photocatalyst and methanol at a flow rate of 200 mLmin for 30 min under magnetic stirring. Then the reactor was totally sealed, and the lamp was turned on.10 h later, the clear solution was separated centrifugally from the slurry and analyzed by hydrogen flame detector (FID) of GC (SCION 456-GC).Blank tests were carried out under the same condition except for no ultraviolet radiation or no catalysts.

catalyst.source: The pure CdS was prepared under the same condition except adding g-C 3 N 4 .
catalyst.context: All of the reagents, urea, cadmium nitrate (Cd(NO 3 ) 2 4H 2 O), thioacetamide, and methanol were commercially available and used without further purification. catalyst.source: After evaluating their activities of photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 and comparing with pure CdS and pure g-C 3 N 4 , we found the photocatalytic activities of the composites to be significantly improved by forming heterojunction.
catalyst.context: The g-C 3 N 4 synthesized by the polycondensation method was combined with CdS by a simple hydrothermal method without using any templates, the obtained CdSg-C 3 N 4 composites consisted of flaky g-C 3 N 4 and uniformly loaded CdS nanospheres.After evaluating their activities of photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 and comparing with pure CdS and pure g-C 3 N 4 , we found the photocatalytic activities of the composites to be significantly improved by forming heterojunction.When the mass ratio of CdS:g-C 3 N 4 was 1:2, the photocatalytic activity reached the highest value of 1352.07 molhg cat .Notably, the CdSg-C 3 N 4 composite photocatalyst showed sufficient stability under illumination, and it could be at least reused for 3 times.Photogenerated electrons transferred from CB of g-C 3 N 4 to neutralize the holes at the VB of CdS, protecting CdS from being oxidatively etched, thus, enhancing the stability of the composites.Conventional band gap theory was used to explain the reaction mechanism. Figure 1.XRD patterns of catalysts: (a) pure g-C3N4, (b) pure CdS, (c) CdSg-C3N4 (1:1), (d) CdSg-C3N4 (1:2), (e) CdSg-C3N4 (1:5). Figure 2. SEM images of (a) pure g-C3N4, (b) pure CdS, (c, d) CdSg-C3N4 (1:2), (e) EDS spectrum of the selected area of CdSg-C3N4 (1:2) sample. Figure 3. TEM images of (a) CdSg-C3N4 (1:5), (b) CdSg-C3N4 (1:2), (c) HRTEM image of CdSg-C3N4 (1:2). Figure 4. (A) UV-vis diffuse reflectance spectra.(B) ( h ) 12 versus the photon energy.(a) CdSg-C3N4 (1:1), (b) CdSg-C3N4 (1:2), (c) CdSg-C3N4 (1:3), (d) CdSg-C3N4 (1:4) and (e) CdSg-C3N4 (1:5). photoca tal ysts Figure 6.Photoluminescence (PL) spectra of (a) g-C3N4, (b) CdSg-C3N4 (1:5), (c) CdSg-C3N4 (1:3) and (d) CdSg-C3N4 (1:2). Figure 7. Reuse times of CdSg-C3N4 (1:2) composite and XRD patterns of fresh and reused CdSg-C3N4 (1:2) composite. Figure 8. Mechanism of the photocatalytic reduction of CO2 in methanol over the CdSg-C3N4 composites.
